title: terebinthus angustata
intro: Terebinthus angustata, also known as the narrow-leaved turpentine bush, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Terebinthus angustata include relief from coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as its ability to soothe and calm the skin. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
